title: Next demo run: tracing + retry behavior recommendation
summary: Keep claim-level trace summaries in the dashboard (with full traces saved to disk) and use a small, explicit retry cap (1 auto-retry only for transient provider errors). This preserves reviewer usefulness and demo responsiveness while staying within finance’s conditional cost approval and avoiding “silent” cost growth.

claims.text: Candidate prompt runs used 11% more tokens but improved latency by 6% mainly because fewer outputs needed manual reruns; the speedup came from fewer repeated attempts, not shorter single generations.

Finance will approve demo-quality eval runs up to the reference target cost budget if the report explains how extra tokens reduce review time; exceeding the budget requires reducing batch size, capping retries, or summarizing traces more aggressively (no approval for unlimited trace retention).
Reviewers find long traces useful only when summarized next to the failed claim and cited source; raw provider payload dumps slow review because reviewers must search for relevant evidence.

For live demo perception, a single briefing felt responsive under 10 seconds, and stakeholders tolerated longer full runs when progress (current case, elapsed time) and artifact paths updated continuously.

Policy direction supports a small retry cap for demo eval runs: allow one automatic retry for transient provider errors and require a manifest entry when retries change cost/latency; it rejects silent repeated generations that make cost harder to explain.

openQuestions: Is the next demo run expected to stay at/under the reference target cost budget, or should we proactively reduce batch size or trace verbosity to ensure compliance with finance’s conditional approval? (S2)
Do we need to optimize single-briefing completion time to consistently land under ~10 seconds, or will progress + artifact-path updates be sufficient for the planned format? (S4)

Should the dashboard display the trace-summary mock fields (selected source ids, missing evidence, retry count, output path) by default, keeping raw payloads only on disk to preserve readability? (S6)
recommendation: Use summarized, claim-adjacent tracing in the UI (keeping full detailed traces on disk) and cap retries to 1 automatic retry for transient errors with explicit manifest logging; keep costs within the reference target budget unless you reduce batch size or further compress trace summaries if over budget.
